DISCOVERY 301, A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER AND TO APPOINT CRAIG A. BLOCK IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWING IF OTHER EFFORTS FAIL AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO TAKE STEPS TO SATISFY THE DEBT. SELLING DEFENDANT'S LIQUOR LICENSE AT THIS POINT IS TOO EXTREME GIVEN THAT IT IS WORTH SEVERAL TIMES PLAINTIFF'S JUDGMENT AND WILL INCAPACITATE DEFENDANT. PURSUANT TO PARTIES STIPULATION, DEFENDANT TO MAKE A DOWN PAYMENT OF $5,000.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN 14 DAYS OR BY MAY 9, 2011 AND A MONTHLY PAYMENT OF $1,000.00 TO BE PAID BY THE 9TH OF EACH MONTH UNTIL PAID. Notice And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DOUGLAS B. PROVENCHER OF PROVENCHER & FLATT LLP, TEL. NO. (707)284-2380 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. MARK RENNIE, TEL. NO. 981-4500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INCORPORATED'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver and to appoint CRAIG A. BLOCK is Denied without prejudice to renewing if other efforts fail and Defendant fails to take steps to satisfy the debt. Selling Defendant's liquor license at this point is too extreme given that it is worth several times Plaintiff's judgment and will incapacitate Defendant. PURSUANT TO PARTIES STIPULATION, DEFENDANT TO MAKE A DOWN PAYMENT OF $5,000.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN 14 DAYS OR BY MAY 9, 2011 AND A MONTHLY PAYMENT OF $1,000.00 TO BE PAID BY THE 9TH OF EACH MONTH UNTIL PAID. MR. PROVENCHER TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER TO WHICH NOTICE WAS WAIVED BY MR. RENNIE. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported = (301/PJB)

Notice And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Admissions And Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Admissions, And Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Special Interrogatories and Form Interrogatories is granted as to Defendant Utid Srisawat. No opposition has been filed. Defendant Utid Srisawat's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant Utid Srisawat is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant Utid Srisawat in the amount of $640, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
